subject: Teams Meeting: Support Team Weekly Standup

analysis.body:
Rachel Kim: Good morning everyone. Let's do our weekly standup. Mike, you're up first.

Agent Mike Rivera: Morning. This week I handled 47 calls. The biggest issue was the billing batch error from February — I had six customers call about duplicate charges. All refunds are processed now. I also saved a cancellation yesterday with the loyalty discount.

Rachel Kim: Great work on the retention. Lisa?

Agent Lisa Park: I had 38 calls, mostly technical support. The VPN 4.2.0 bug drove a lot of volume — about 15 calls were related to that. Since the 4.2.1 patch went out, those have dropped to zero. I also did some proactive follow-ups which got very positive feedback.

Rachel Kim: Love the proactive approach. Tom, what's the data telling us?

Tom Bradley: Overall metrics look good. Average handle time is down 12% week over week. Customer satisfaction score is at 4.3 out of 5. The fraud detection process memo is drafted and going to legal review today. One concern — we're seeing an uptick in cancellation inquiries. Competitor X launched a price cut last week.

Rachel Kim: That's important. Mike, you mentioned the loyalty discount worked. Let's formalize that as a standard retention offer. Tom, can you pull the data on how many customers would qualify?

Tom Bradley: Sure, I'll have that by end of day.

Rachel Kim: Perfect. Any blockers anyone?

Agent Lisa Park: We could use better documentation on the new API integration. I'm getting questions I can't answer.

Rachel Kim: Noted. I'll follow up with engineering. Alright, great standup everyone. Let's have a strong week.

Weekly support standup. Mike: 47 calls, billing batch error resolved, cancellation saved. Lisa: 38 calls, VPN bug resolved with 4.2.1 patch, proactive follow-ups. Tom: AHT down 12%, CSAT 4.3/5, fraud detection memo in legal review, competitor price cut driving cancellation uptick. Action items: formalize loyalty discount as retention offer, pull qualification data, improve API docs.
